catalyst.source: In this study, photocatalytic activities of BaZrO 3 for CO 2 reduction were investigated in detail.
catalyst.context: In this study, photocatalytic activities of BaZrO 3 for CO 2 reduction were investigated in detail.BaZrO 3 samples were successfully obtained via a Pechini process at different temperatures.The prepared samples were characterized by X-ray diffraction (XRD), BET surface area measurement, UV-vis absorption spectroscopy, scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM).The effects of the deposition of various metal nanoparticles on the surface of BaZrO 3 were also discussed.The results proved that the highly dispersed silver nanoparticles play a crucial role in improving the photocatalytic activities of BaZrO 3 .

catalyst.source: The photocatalytic properties of BaZrO 3 as a new photocatalyst for CO 2 reduction have been investigated.
catalyst.context: The photocatalytic properties of BaZrO 3 as a new photocatalyst for CO 2 reduction have been investigated.The BaZrO 3 samples obtained at 1273 K with 0.3 wt Ag nanoparticles deposited showed the highest photocatalytic activities.Under UV light irradiation, the Ag nanoparticle deposited BaZrO 3 showed higher photocatalytic activities than the samples with other metal nanoparticles deposited.The deposition of Ag nanoparticles on BaZrO 3 results in more efficient separation of the photogenerated electron-hole pairs, and the electron transmission mechanism should be further investigated in detail to enhance the photocatalytic activity of BaZrO 3 . Fig. 1 XRD patterns of BaZrO 3 synthesized at 1173, 1273, 1373 and 1473 K. Fig. 2 The products were detected in three blank reactions and the CH 4 yield of pure BaZrO 3 prepared at different temperatures (1273, 1373 and 1473 K). Fig. 3 CH 4 yields of BaZrO 3 deposited with different cocatalysts (Ru, Cu, Au, Pt, Ag) having the same apparent amount. Fig. 4 CH 4 yields of BaZrO 3 with different amounts of Ag deposition. Fig. 5 (a) SEM microphotograph of BaZrO 3 deposited with 0.3 wt Ag.(b) TEM pattern and (c) HRTEM pattern of 0.3 wt Ag deposited BaZrO 3 . This journal is The Royal Society of Chemistry 2015 This work is supported by the National Basic Research Program of China (973 Program, 2013CB632404), a project funded by the Priority Academic Program Development of Jiangsu Higher Education Institutions, New Century Excellent Talents in University (NCET-12-0268), the National Natural Science Foundation of China (no.51272102 and 21103070), and the Open Research Fund of Hunan Key Laboratory of Applied Environmental Photocatalysis (ccsu-KF-1404), Changsa University.

co-catalyst.source: The results proved that the highly dispersed silver nanoparticles play a crucial role in improving the photocatalytic activities of BaZrO 3 .
co-catalyst.context: In this study, photocatalytic activities of BaZrO 3 for CO 2 reduction were investigated in detail.BaZrO 3 samples were successfully obtained via a Pechini process at different temperatures.The prepared samples were characterized by X-ray diffraction (XRD), BET surface area measurement, UV-vis absorption spectroscopy, scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM).The effects of the deposition of various metal nanoparticles on the surface of BaZrO 3 were also discussed.The results proved that the highly dispersed silver nanoparticles play a crucial role in improving the photocatalytic activities of BaZrO 3 .

co-catalyst.source: Noble metals (Ag, Au, Pt) were deposited on the surface of BaZrO 3 as the different cocatalysts through liquid phase chemical reduction according to a reported method.
co-catalyst.context: The BaZrO 3 samples were prepared using the same method as that reported by our previous paper. 26Stoichiometric amounts of ZrIJNO 3 ) 4 J5H 2 O and BaIJNO 3 ) 2 (both are of analytical reagent grade) were initially prepared in a beaker.Then, a mixture of 60 wt citric acid monohydrate IJC 6 H 8 O 7 , purity 99.9) and 40 wt ethylene glycol IJC 2 H 6 O 2 , purity 99.9) (the molar ratio of citric acid to total cations was fixed at 2 : 1) was added to the beaker.Subsequently a solution was acquired by adding distilled water to the beaker.This solution was homogenized by stirring at room temperature for about 1 h.Then the prepared clear solution was evaporated in a baking oven at 343 K until a brown resin was formed.The obtained resin was heated for 6 h at 673 K to remove the majority of the organics.After that, the obtained solid was ground using an agate mortar, and finally thermally sintered at different temperatures (1173, 1273, 1373, 1473 K) for 10 h under air atmosphere. Noble metals (Ag, Au, Pt) were deposited on the surface of BaZrO 3 as the different cocatalysts through liquid phase chemical reduction according to a reported method. 19A cation solution (AgNO 3 , HAuCl 4 , H 2 PtCl 6 ) of 0.1 mol L -1 was added to 30 ml of an aqueous suspension containing 0.2 g of BaZrO 3 .After adding an equimolar amount of NaH 2 PO 2 (0.4 mol L -1 ) with respect to the cation in the suspension, the mixture was stirred at 333 K for 1 h.The obtained mixture was washed with distilled water and dried at 343 K in air.Then the sample was annealed at 573 K for 1 h. 29he deposition of Cu and Ru cocatalysts was carried out via an impregnation method.The corresponding cation solution (CuCl 2 , IJNH 4 ) 2 RuCl 6 ) and calcination at 723 K for 1 h were selected as the conditions of the impregnation method. The reduction treatment was carried out under H 2 gas flow at 573 K for 2 h. The data of the crystal structure and the phase purity were collected by a powder X-ray diffractometer (Ultima III, Rigaku, Japan) using Cu K radiation.The acceleration voltage was 40 kV, and the emission current was 40 mA.The diffractograms were recorded in the 2 range of 20to 80at a scan rate of 10min -1 .The UV-vis reflectance spectra were recorded with a UV-vis spectrophotometer (UV-2550, Shimadzu, Japan) using BaSO 4 as the background.The specific surface area of the BaZrO 3 samples was determined by BET measurement (TriStar-3000, Micromeritics, USA) using nitrogen adsorption at 77 K. Transmission electron microscopy (JEOL 3010) was employed to characterize the surface morphologies of BaZrO 3 powder and silver nanoparticles.The gas products were detected using a gas chromatograph with a double-FID (GC-2014, Shimadzu, Japan). The photocatalytic reaction was executed in a cylindrical quartz cell.A plate glass loader filled with the fresh sample (0.1 g) was placed in the cell.The reaction system was connected with a traditional vacuum system and stabilized at ambient temperature by flowing cool water.After the sample was loaded, the reaction system was evacuated to about 1 mTorr using a mechanical pump and then sealed.Carbon dioxide (99.99 pure) was transmitted into the reaction system until the pressure reached 93.5 kPa.Before adding 0.4 ml of distilled water into the reaction system, the distilled water was heated at 100 C in order to drive out the dissolved O 2 .Then, the sample absorbed CO 2 and water vapor for 8 h in the dark with the gas circulator working.Before illumination, 1 ml of the gas sample was withdrawn and injected into the gas chromatograph by using a gas-tight syringe (1 ml) and the analysis was used as the reference. In the experiments, a 300 W Xe lamp (PE 300BUV) was used as the light source.The whole reaction system was tightly closed during the irradiation.According to the analysis using the gas chromatograph, methane was the major hydrocarbon in all these experiments.Therefore, the direct measure of activity toward CO 2 photoreduction was referenced to the methane yield.To make sure that the hydrocarbon products were attributed to the photoreduction of CO 2 , three contrast tests were carried out.The first one was UV-illuminated without the catalyst, the second one was the dark reaction under the same experimental conditions and the third one was illuminated with the photocatalyst in the absence of CO 2 .All the reactions have been repeated more than five times to confirm the reproducibility and consistency of the photocatalytic experiment.

Fig. 1 XRD patterns of BaZrO 3 synthesized at 1173, 1273, 1373 and 1473 K. Fig. 2 The products were detected in three blank reactions and the CH 4 yield of pure BaZrO 3 prepared at different temperatures (1273, 1373 and 1473 K). Fig. 3 CH 4 yields of BaZrO 3 deposited with different cocatalysts (Ru, Cu, Au, Pt, Ag) having the same apparent amount. Fig. 4 CH 4 yields of BaZrO 3 with different amounts of Ag deposition. Fig. 5 (a) SEM microphotograph of BaZrO 3 deposited with 0.3 wt Ag.(b) TEM pattern and (c) HRTEM pattern of 0.3 wt Ag deposited BaZrO 3 . light source.source: In the experiments, a 300 W Xe lamp (PE 300BUV) was used as the light source.
light source.context: The BaZrO 3 samples were prepared using the same method as that reported by our previous paper. 26Stoichiometric amounts of ZrIJNO 3 ) 4 J5H 2 O and BaIJNO 3 ) 2 (both are of analytical reagent grade) were initially prepared in a beaker.Then, a mixture of 60 wt citric acid monohydrate IJC 6 H 8 O 7 , purity 99.9) and 40 wt ethylene glycol IJC 2 H 6 O 2 , purity 99.9) (the molar ratio of citric acid to total cations was fixed at 2 : 1) was added to the beaker.Subsequently a solution was acquired by adding distilled water to the beaker.This solution was homogenized by stirring at room temperature for about 1 h.Then the prepared clear solution was evaporated in a baking oven at 343 K until a brown resin was formed.The obtained resin was heated for 6 h at 673 K to remove the majority of the organics.After that, the obtained solid was ground using an agate mortar, and finally thermally sintered at different temperatures (1173, 1273, 1373, 1473 K) for 10 h under air atmosphere. Noble metals (Ag, Au, Pt) were deposited on the surface of BaZrO 3 as the different cocatalysts through liquid phase chemical reduction according to a reported method. 19A cation solution (AgNO 3 , HAuCl 4 , H 2 PtCl 6 ) of 0.1 mol L -1 was added to 30 ml of an aqueous suspension containing 0.2 g of BaZrO 3 .After adding an equimolar amount of NaH 2 PO 2 (0.4 mol L -1 ) with respect to the cation in the suspension, the mixture was stirred at 333 K for 1 h.The obtained mixture was washed with distilled water and dried at 343 K in air.Then the sample was annealed at 573 K for 1 h. 29he deposition of Cu and Ru cocatalysts was carried out via an impregnation method.The corresponding cation solution (CuCl 2 , IJNH 4 ) 2 RuCl 6 ) and calcination at 723 K for 1 h were selected as the conditions of the impregnation method. The reduction treatment was carried out under H 2 gas flow at 573 K for 2 h. The data of the crystal structure and the phase purity were collected by a powder X-ray diffractometer (Ultima III, Rigaku, Japan) using Cu K radiation.The acceleration voltage was 40 kV, and the emission current was 40 mA.The diffractograms were recorded in the 2 range of 20to 80at a scan rate of 10min -1 .The UV-vis reflectance spectra were recorded with a UV-vis spectrophotometer (UV-2550, Shimadzu, Japan) using BaSO 4 as the background.The specific surface area of the BaZrO 3 samples was determined by BET measurement (TriStar-3000, Micromeritics, USA) using nitrogen adsorption at 77 K. Transmission electron microscopy (JEOL 3010) was employed to characterize the surface morphologies of BaZrO 3 powder and silver nanoparticles.The gas products were detected using a gas chromatograph with a double-FID (GC-2014, Shimadzu, Japan). The photocatalytic reaction was executed in a cylindrical quartz cell.A plate glass loader filled with the fresh sample (0.1 g) was placed in the cell.The reaction system was connected with a traditional vacuum system and stabilized at ambient temperature by flowing cool water.After the sample was loaded, the reaction system was evacuated to about 1 mTorr using a mechanical pump and then sealed.Carbon dioxide (99.99 pure) was transmitted into the reaction system until the pressure reached 93.5 kPa.Before adding 0.4 ml of distilled water into the reaction system, the distilled water was heated at 100 C in order to drive out the dissolved O 2 .Then, the sample absorbed CO 2 and water vapor for 8 h in the dark with the gas circulator working.Before illumination, 1 ml of the gas sample was withdrawn and injected into the gas chromatograph by using a gas-tight syringe (1 ml) and the analysis was used as the reference. In the experiments, a 300 W Xe lamp (PE 300BUV) was used as the light source.The whole reaction system was tightly closed during the irradiation.According to the analysis using the gas chromatograph, methane was the major hydrocarbon in all these experiments.Therefore, the direct measure of activity toward CO 2 photoreduction was referenced to the methane yield.To make sure that the hydrocarbon products were attributed to the photoreduction of CO 2 , three contrast tests were carried out.The first one was UV-illuminated without the catalyst, the second one was the dark reaction under the same experimental conditions and the third one was illuminated with the photocatalyst in the absence of CO 2 .All the reactions have been repeated more than five times to confirm the reproducibility and consistency of the photocatalytic experiment.

lamp.source: Under UV light irradiation, the Ag nanoparticle deposited BaZrO 3 showed higher photocatalytic activities than the samples with other metal nanoparticles deposited.
lamp.context: The photocatalytic properties of BaZrO 3 as a new photocatalyst for CO 2 reduction have been investigated.The BaZrO 3 samples obtained at 1273 K with 0.3 wt Ag nanoparticles deposited showed the highest photocatalytic activities.Under UV light irradiation, the Ag nanoparticle deposited BaZrO 3 showed higher photocatalytic activities than the samples with other metal nanoparticles deposited.The deposition of Ag nanoparticles on BaZrO 3 results in more efficient separation of the photogenerated electron-hole pairs, and the electron transmission mechanism should be further investigated in detail to enhance the photocatalytic activity of BaZrO 3 . Fig. 1 XRD patterns of BaZrO 3 synthesized at 1173, 1273, 1373 and 1473 K. Fig. 2 The products were detected in three blank reactions and the CH 4 yield of pure BaZrO 3 prepared at different temperatures (1273, 1373 and 1473 K). Fig. 3 CH 4 yields of BaZrO 3 deposited with different cocatalysts (Ru, Cu, Au, Pt, Ag) having the same apparent amount. Fig. 4 CH 4 yields of BaZrO 3 with different amounts of Ag deposition. Fig. 5 (a) SEM microphotograph of BaZrO 3 deposited with 0.3 wt Ag.(b) TEM pattern and (c) HRTEM pattern of 0.3 wt Ag deposited BaZrO 3 .
